title: Institutional pathways of anticipatory action and their effects on disaster governance effectiveness : A comparative study of the Philippines and Bangladesh
content: This thesis examines how different forms and degrees of institutionalization of anticipatory action influence the effectiveness of disaster governance, conceptualized along the dimensions of timeliness and coherence of the Philippines and Bangladesh.
snippet: This thesis examines how different forms and degrees of institutionalization of anticipatory action influence the effectiveness of disaster governance, conceptualized along the dimensions of

title: Wildfire smoke threatens the World Cup final. FIFA still doesn’t have a plan.
content: There’s no way to close off the open-air stadium where stars Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal are slated to square off.
snippet: There’s no way to close off the open-air stadium where stars Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal are slated to square off.

title: The 24 June 2026 Venezuela earthquakes
content: This report constitutes a synthesis of field observations collected during the Greek scientific mission in Venezuela, published information obtained from international scientific organizations, governmental agencies, and the scientific literature.
snippet: This report constitutes a synthesis of field observations collected during the Greek scientific mission in Venezuela, published information obtained from international scientific organizations,

title: Strengthening provincial coherence between climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction and management for inclusive and resilient development in Nepal
content: The main objective of this policy brief is to offer evidence-based and concise findings, and policy recommendations to strengthen coherence between CCA and DRRM at the Provincial Level in Nepal.
snippet: The main objective of this policy brief is to offer evidence-based and concise findings, and policy recommendations to strengthen coherence between CCA and DRRM at the Provincial Level in Nepal.

title: Exploring the impact mechanisms of tropical cyclone losses: a global analysis integrating hazard, exposure, and vulnerability (1990–2019)
content: This study examines how hazard intensity, exposure and vulnerability shape tropical cyclone mortality and direct economic losses worldwide, using globally recorded disaster loss data from 1990 to 2019.
snippet: This study examines how hazard intensity, exposure and vulnerability shape tropical cyclone mortality and direct economic losses worldwide, using globally recorded disaster loss data from 1990 to

title: RFP_Review & Assurance Opinion-CDRI Trust fund mechanism design
content: The purpose of this assignment is to provide an independent, objective assessment of the adequacy of the design of CDRI's Trust Fund mechanism.
snippet: The purpose of this assignment is to provide an independent, objective assessment of the adequacy of the design of CDRI's Trust Fund mechanism.

title: Learning from disaster: evaluating Pakistan's first national adaptation plan for flood risk management
content: This article critically reviews Pakistan's first National Adaptation Plan (NAP) and draws lessons from past major flood events, including the 2010 and 2022 floods, to assess the effectiveness of current flood risk management strategies.
snippet: This article critically reviews Pakistan's first National Adaptation Plan (NAP) and draws lessons from past major flood events, including the 2010 and 2022 floods, to assess the effectiveness of

title: When disaster recovery becomes a way of life: Community disaster fatigue is on the rise with more frequent floods
content: Flash flooding has been tearing up communities across the U.S., with heavy downpours sending creeks and rivers rushing over their banks from Texas to Kentucky , across the Midwest and into the Mid-Atlantic states and the Northeast.
snippet: Flash flooding has been tearing up communities across the U.S., with heavy downpours sending creeks and rivers rushing over their banks from Texas to Kentucky , across the Midwest and into the

title: Positioning Nepal to access the fund for responding to loss and damage: recommendations to untap essential finance
content: This policy brief examines Nepal's opportunity to leverage the Barbados Implementation Modalities (BIM) start-up phase to access the Fund for responding to Loss and Damage (FRLD).
snippet: This policy brief examines Nepal's opportunity to leverage the Barbados Implementation Modalities (BIM) start-up phase to access the Fund for responding to Loss and Damage (FRLD).

title: Climate impacts and public support for government action in Africa
content: This 14-country African study finds that perceived climate impacts significantly boost public support for government action, meaning the challenge is translating existing demand into effective policy—not creating it.
snippet: This 14-country African study finds that perceived climate impacts significantly boost public support for government action, meaning the challenge is translating existing demand into effective

title: Update on PPED treaty progress
content: This Power Presentation presents updates regarding the incorporation of displacement related issues into the Treaty for the Protection of Persons in the event of Disasters.
snippet: This Power Presentation presents updates regarding the incorporation of displacement related issues into the Treaty for the Protection of Persons in the event of Disasters.

title: Policy brief 17: Foregrounding displacement and evacuations in the proposed treaty on the protection of persons in the event of disasters
content: This policy brief examines the increasing challenge of disaster-related displacement and argues that the proposed Treaty on the Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED Treaty) should explicitly address the protection of displaced people.
snippet: This policy brief examines the increasing challenge of disaster-related displacement and argues that the proposed Treaty on the Protection of Persons in the Event of Disasters (PPED Treaty) should

title: Technical briefing summary report: Treaty on the protection of persons in the event of disasters and disaster displacement
content: This summary report presents the key insights from the technical briefing and a summary of the presentations and exchange among participants.
snippet: This summary report presents the key insights from the technical briefing and a summary of the presentations and exchange among participants.

title: Impacts of extreme climate events on megacities in West Africa: A case of the Greater Accra Region
content: This study investigates the perceived impacts of extreme climate events particularly floods, heatwaves, and droughts on food security, public health, energy systems, and water resources in the Greater Accra Region (GAR), Ghana.
snippet: This study investigates the perceived impacts of extreme climate events particularly floods, heatwaves, and droughts on food security, public health, energy systems, and water resources in the Greater

title: Scoring the compliance of cell broadcast alert messages: Application in France and insights for authorities and future research
content: This study assesses cell broadcast alert messages in France, showing gaps between recommended practice and real use, and highlighting how clearer, more complete message content can improve public warning and protective action.
snippet: This study assesses cell broadcast alert messages in France, showing gaps between recommended practice and real use, and highlighting how clearer, more complete message content can improve public

title: Incorporating displacement into the treaty on the protection of persons in the event of disasters (PPED Treaty)
content: This two-pages resource brief provides concrete recommendations and entry points to incorporate displacement into the Treaty on the Protection of Persons in the event of Disasters.
snippet: This two-pages resource brief provides concrete recommendations and entry points to incorporate displacement into the Treaty on the Protection of Persons in the event of Disasters.

title: Towards a legally-binding convention on the protection of persons in the event of disasters
content: This guide introduces the ongoing process to develop a legally binding convention on the protection of persons in the event of disasters (PPED)  and explains why feminist and gender-justice advocates need to be paying attention.
snippet: This guide introduces the ongoing process to develop a legally binding convention on the protection of persons in the event of disasters (PPED) and explains why feminist and gender-justice advocates

title: Himalayan snowfall has been miscalculated for years, here’s how we found a new way to measure it
content: Mountain snow and meltwater from glaciers in the Himalayas determine how much water is available for drinking, farming and hydropower for millions of people in India, Pakistan, Nepal, China and Afghanistan.
snippet: Mountain snow and meltwater from glaciers in the Himalayas determine how much water is available for drinking, farming and hydropower for millions of people in India, Pakistan, Nepal, China and

title: Hazard: Wildfire
content: Wildfires are any unplanned or uncontrolled fire affecting natural, cultural, industrial and residential landscapes.
snippet: Wildfires are any unplanned or uncontrolled fire affecting natural, cultural, industrial and residential landscapes.

title: A ‘crisis communication gap’ threatens Indigenous peoples
content: Most emergency alerts and warnings are not provided in languages spoken by Indigenous peoples, placing them at greater risk during fires, floods, and other disasters.
snippet: Most emergency alerts and warnings are not provided in languages spoken by Indigenous peoples, placing them at greater risk during fires, floods, and other disasters.

title: Associations of emotional divergence in risk communication with forecast error type during typhoon Khanun
content: This study examines how rainfall forecast errors during Typhoon Khanun shaped public emotions online, showing that underestimated and overestimated forecasts can increase anxiety, confusion and fatigue in risk communication.
snippet: This study examines how rainfall forecast errors during Typhoon Khanun shaped public emotions online, showing that underestimated and overestimated forecasts can increase anxiety, confusion and

title: Pacific emergency broadcasting: vital but critically underfunded
content: This article examines emergency communication in the Pacific, highlighting how national broadcasters are relied on during disasters but often lack the resources, infrastructure and formal support needed to stay on air.
snippet: This article examines emergency communication in the Pacific, highlighting how national broadcasters are relied on during disasters but often lack the resources, infrastructure and formal support

title: Support for climate and biodiversity policies differs across cultural worldviews in Japan
content: This study examines how cultural worldviews shape support for climate and biodiversity policies in Japan, finding that biodiversity conservation may face less worldview-driven resistance than climate action.
snippet: This study examines how cultural worldviews shape support for climate and biodiversity policies in Japan, finding that biodiversity conservation may face less worldview-driven resistance than climate

title: Wildfire portal: Risk communication
content: Effective risk communication is essential for helping people understand wildfire threats, make informed decisions and take timely action before, during and after a fire.
snippet: Effective risk communication is essential for helping people understand wildfire threats, make informed decisions and take timely action before, during and after a fire.

title: Wildfire portal: Resilient development
content: Resilient development integrates wildfire risk into the way communities are planned, built and managed, reducing exposure and vulnerability before disasters occur.
snippet: Resilient development integrates wildfire risk into the way communities are planned, built and managed, reducing exposure and vulnerability before disasters occur.

title: Wildfire portal: Early warning
content: Early warning systems are essential for reducing the impacts of wildfires by enabling timely action and helping communities prepare, respond and protect lives and livelihoods.
snippet: Early warning systems are essential for reducing the impacts of wildfires by enabling timely action and helping communities prepare, respond and protect lives and livelihoods.

title: Wildfire portal: Water
content: Wildfires can affect water resources by damaging watersheds, degrading water quality and disrupting water supply systems.
snippet: Wildfires can affect water resources by damaging watersheds, degrading water quality and disrupting water supply systems.

title: Wildfire portal: Food security and agriculture
content: Wildfires can have significant impacts on food security and agriculture by damaging crops, livestock, forests and agricultural infrastructure.
snippet: Wildfires can have significant impacts on food security and agriculture by damaging crops, livestock, forests and agricultural infrastructure.

title: Wildfire portal: Education
content: Wildfires can disrupt education systems by damaging schools, interrupting learning and affecting the wellbeing of students, teachers and communities.
snippet: Wildfires can disrupt education systems by damaging schools, interrupting learning and affecting the wellbeing of students, teachers and communities.

title: Wildfire portal: Evacuation and displacement
content: Wildfires can force people to leave their homes with little notice, leading to both short-term evacuations and longer-term displacement.
snippet: Wildfires can force people to leave their homes with little notice, leading to both short-term evacuations and longer-term displacement.

title: Wildfire portal: Public health
content: Wildfires pose serious risks to public health, both during and long after a fire.
snippet: Wildfires pose serious risks to public health, both during and long after a fire.

title: Wildfire portal: Ecosystem-based approaches
content: Ecosystem-based approaches reduce wildfire risk by protecting, restoring and managing landscapes in ways that strengthen their natural resilience to fire.
snippet: Ecosystem-based approaches reduce wildfire risk by protecting, restoring and managing landscapes in ways that strengthen their natural resilience to fire.

title: Wildfire Portal
content: The Wildfire Portal brings together trusted knowledge, data, and practical experience on wildfires for audiences working across research, policy, and practice.
snippet: The Wildfire Portal brings together trusted knowledge, data, and practical experience on wildfires for audiences working across research, policy, and practice.

title: Thousands flee as First Nations bear the brunt of Canada’s wildfires
content: More than 900 wildfires are burning throughout Canada, with devastating impacts for First Nations. Thirteen Nations remain under evacuation orders that have forced 2,182 people from their homes.
snippet: More than 900 wildfires are burning throughout Canada, with devastating impacts for First Nations. Thirteen Nations remain under evacuation orders that have forced 2,182 people from their homes.

title: Climate disaster resilience requires more than insurance
content: Climate-related disasters are becoming one of Asia’s most important economic and fiscal challenges.
snippet: Climate-related disasters are becoming one of Asia’s most important economic and fiscal challenges.

title: Intercity connectivity enhances urban mobility resilience to extreme rainfall
content: This research article examines how intercity connectivity strengthens urban mobility resilience during extreme rainfall, using mobility data from 366 Chinese cities.
snippet: This research article examines how intercity connectivity strengthens urban mobility resilience during extreme rainfall, using mobility data from 366 Chinese cities.

title: Latest precipitation models still seem to underestimate risk as ‘1,000-year’ rain events Pummel Texas
content: The Nueces River of South Texas flowed at twice the rate of Niagara Falls last week during the region’s second flood disaster in two years.
snippet: The Nueces River of South Texas flowed at twice the rate of Niagara Falls last week during the region’s second flood disaster in two years.

title: Building dams to protect Lismore from flooding would cost billions. Could nature do it better?
content: In anticipation of future flooding, Australia could combine traditional structural and flood mitigation measures, such as dams and levees, with nature-inspired designs.
snippet: In anticipation of future flooding, Australia could combine traditional structural and flood mitigation measures, such as dams and levees, with nature-inspired designs.

title: Climate change and urban pressures intensify flood risk on the Gulf of Guinea coast
content: Between 20–22 June 2026, the coastal regions of the countries bordering the Gulf of Guinea, particularly Côte d’Ivoire, Ghana, Togo, and Nigeria, experienced exceptionally widespread and severe flooding following intense and persistent rainfall.
snippet: Between 20–22 June 2026, the coastal regions of the countries bordering the Gulf of Guinea, particularly Côte d’Ivoire, Ghana, Togo, and Nigeria, experienced exceptionally widespread and severe

title: The truncated toolkit: disaster risk finance and the limits of autonomy in the Dutch Caribbean
content: This working paper examines how Aruba, Curaçao and Sint Maarten finance disaster recovery and why they make limited use of modern disaster risk financing instruments despite their high exposure to natural hazards.
